purpose: Make the current SQL/JPA knowledge layer inspectable, expose normalized schema-detail metadata for supported proof paths, and wire covered generation paths to local facts before asking an LLM/provider.

deterministic_commands: python3 main.py --command schema_detail --db example.db --table users --format json

topic_matrix.notes: Keeps runtime capability claims separate from dialect language facts.
Classifies core read/write statements and CoQuery support boundaries.
Provides table/source introspection templates or JPA source-command guidance.

Exposes normalized columns, primary keys, foreign keys, indexes, constraints, and SQLite create SQL for verified SQLite plus mocked and smoke-testable PostgreSQL paths; MySQL and JPQL remain guidance only.
Separates SQL LIMIT/OFFSET templates from JPQL API-level pagination.
Captures driver/API placeholder conventions for basic generated examples.
Covers common scalar mappings; not yet a complete DDL or conversion matrix.
Covers common comparison, logical, pattern, null, and membership operators.
Provides basic SQL joins and JPQL relationship/fetch join patterns.
Covers common database constraints and first JPA mapping annotations.
Documents dialect templates and marks unsupported CoQuery execution paths.
Defines read-only default, explicit write gate, and risky UPDATE/DELETE warnings.

can_answer_locally: which SQL/JPA capability claims are safe
which common statements are reads or writes
basic schema introspection templates by supported/planned dialect
normalized schema-detail metadata for SQLite and the PostgreSQL proof path
common operator, join, constraint, and upsert patterns
JPQL vs SQL naming and runtime boundaries
simple natural-language SQL drafting without provider calls
generation and write planning context for dialect and safety rules
still_needs_llm_or_external_docs: vendor-specific function coverage beyond the seed topics

implemented_gates.name: local_knowledge_first_generation
implemented_gates.description: Generation, natural, and write planning paths now attach local DB/JPA knowledge context before provider use; simple covered natural requests skip provider calls.

implemented_gates.name: schema_detail_knowledge
implemented_gates.description: CoQuery can expose normalized column, primary-key, foreign-key, index, constraint, and SQLite create-SQL metadata through schema_detail, with local DB knowledge topics documenting the path.

remaining_gaps.description: Local knowledge is consulted before covered generation paths, and schema-detail metadata can be queried, but generated SQL templates do not yet validate identifiers against schema_detail output.
remaining_gaps.next_step: Use schema_detail output to select dialect-specific templates and validate table/column identifiers before returning generated SQL.

remaining_gaps.description: Date/time, string, aggregate, JSON, and null-handling function differences are not yet structured.
remaining_gaps.next_step: Add source-linked function rules per dialect.

remaining_gaps.description: Transaction, isolation, locking, and dry-run behavior are not yet represented.
remaining_gaps.next_step: Add transaction safety rules before broadening write workflows.

remaining_gaps.description: JPQL knowledge covers boundaries and common patterns but does not generate or execute JPQL.
remaining_gaps.next_step: Add a Java/JPA runner proof before claiming JPQL execution support.

remaining_gaps.description: Official source refresh cadence is documented informally, not enforced by tooling.
remaining_gaps.next_step: Add a source-audit checklist or metadata gate for knowledge changes.

next_gate.name: schema_detail_aware_generation
next_gate.pass_condition: CoQuery generation and natural-language drafting can consume schema_detail metadata to validate identifiers and shape safe SELECT templates.
next_gate.verification: Add tests showing generated SQL uses schema_detail output for table and column validation without provider calls on covered simple requests.
